results.evidence: SEARCHABLE_STATUSES excludes 'completed'; test asserts no search call
SEARCHABLE_STATUSES excludes 'abandoned'; test asserts no search call
SEARCHABLE_STATUSES includes 'active'; test asserts search called
All 6 statuses in SEARCHABLE_STATUSES with dedicated tests for each (active, planning, in-progress, backlog, ready_for_review, paused)
SEARCHABLE_STATUSES excludes 'created'; test asserts no search call
Negative cache write in scanIncrements after empty search; test verifies writeFile with correct JSON
Guard metadata.github?.noIssuesFound; test verifies search skipped
Guard state.userStoryIssues.length === 0; test verifies no negative cache on results
Guard !state.mainIssue; test verifies no negative cache when main issue exists
Static searchCache Map on GitHubClientV2; set after CLI call; test verifies cache miss
TTL check with CACHE_TTL_MS; test verifies second call within 30s uses cache
Same TTL logic; test advances Date.now by 31s and verifies fresh API call
MAX_SEARCHES_PER_SCAN=20; guard skips at limit; test asserts exactly 20 calls for 21 increments
Metadata-backed increments still pushed to results; test verifies scanned >= 1 when budget exhausted
Logger.log called with skip count after loop; format: 'Skipped N search(es) due to budget exhaustion (limit: 20)'
Local variable resets per scanIncrements call; test verifies across two reconcile() calls

findings.issue: Pre-existing: fromRepo mock omits checkRateLimit. Production code calls it, throws, and empty catch swallows it.
findings.suggestion: Add checkRateLimit to fromRepo mock in a follow-up increment.
findings.impact: If catch block is ever tightened, all reconciler tests break.

findings.issue: Negative cache persists forever with no TTL. If issues created outside SpecWeave after cache written, never discovered.
findings.suggestion: Consider 24h TTL check on searchedAt in a follow-up. Manual override: delete metadata.github.noIssuesFound.
findings.impact: Silent data staleness for manual issue creation. Mitigated by reconcile --force.
